description: An immutable event record representing the transfer of a fraction of an activity claim between two parties. Multiple transfer events are aggregated to compute the current fractional ownership state of a claim. This record is only to be trusted if created by a trusted verifier handle.

main.description: A single, immutable transfer event for a fraction of an activity claim. Records the sender, recipient, amount, and cryptographic proof of the transfer.

activityClaimUri.description: The AT-URI of the activity claim that this transfer event is associated with.

from.description: The DID of the sender. Omit this field if the sender wishes to remain anonymous.

to.description: The DID of the recipient of the transfer.

amount.description: The amount transferred as a numeric string (e.g. '1000.50').

currency.description: The fiat currency of the transfer (e.g. USD, EUR).

signedAt.description: The timestamp embedded in the signed message payload. This value is part of the data that was cryptographically signed and must match the signing time used during signature generation.

nonce.description: The nonce included in the signed message, used to prevent replay attacks.

chainId.description: The EVM chain ID on which this transfer was signed (e.g. '1' for Ethereum mainnet, '42161' for Arbitrum).

signerEVMAddress.description: The EVM address of the signer, in checksummed hex format with a 0x prefix (e.g. 0xAbCd...1234). Required for signature verification.

signature.description: The cryptographic signature over the transfer message payload.

createdAt.description: Client-declared timestamp when this record was originally created.
